steps.title: Find the integer values of y
steps.content:
From the figure, triangle $ABC$ has base $AC = 12$ on the horizontal axis and a vertical height $BC = y$, so its area is:
$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 12 \cdot y = 6y$$
The constraint $48 \leq 6y \leq 60$ gives $8 \leq y \leq 10$. Since $y$ is an integer, $y \in \{8, 9, 10\}$.

steps.title: Express x in terms of y
steps.content:
From the figure, $x$ is the height of an inner segment at a horizontal distance of $5$ from $A$ along the base, drawn up to line $AB$. Line $AB$ runs from $A(0, 0)$ to $B(12, y)$, so its slope is $\frac{y}{12}$ and its equation is:
$$Y = \frac{y}{12} \cdot X$$
At $X = 5$, the height on line $AB$ is:
$$x = \frac{5y}{12}$$

steps.title: Compute x for each valid y
steps.content:
Substitute each integer value of $y$ into $x = \frac{5y}{12}$:
  • $y = 8$: $x = \frac{40}{12} = \frac{10}{3} \approx 3.33$
  • $y = 9$: $x = \frac{45}{12} = \frac{15}{4} = 3.75$
  • $y = 10$: $x = \frac{50}{12} = \frac{25}{6} \approx 4.17$
Any one of $\frac{10}{3}$, $\frac{15}{4}$, or $\frac{25}{6}$ is a valid answer.
